title: Remove targetless evals: no implicit DOM context
desc: EvalAsync calls without an explicit DOM node or selector target should be removed, not redirected. A CDP eval that does not know which node/frame it operates on is undefined behavior — silent wrong-tab or wrong-frame execution. Refuse and delete, do not convert to find.
steps: Identify evals with no DOM anchor: Runtime.evaluate(js) with no nodeId/objectId/selector context
If the intent is clear (e.g. 'read this editor's text'), rewrite to querySelector+property access with explicit selector
If the intent is NOT clear (legacy fire-and-forget side effects), delete the eval entirely
Never convert targetless eval to a generic find — that just hides the ambiguity
Guard rule: every EvalAsync call site must have a comment stating WHICH element/scope it targets and WHY
author: claude
